PermitPulse workflow

How PermitPulse Checks a Property

A first-pass public-record workflow for one address, built to reduce surprises before you buy, bid, build, list, quote, or promise a timeline.

Order Permit Review Plus View Sample Report Text an Address
ScopeOne address
InputsPublic records
OutputDecision Snapshot
RuleManual confirmation flags
Section 1

Official Sources First

PermitPulse prioritizes official city, county, planning, permit, assessor, parcel, mapping, and housing/code records where available. When a source is missing, blocked, incomplete, or needs direct agency confirmation, the report should say so plainly instead of pretending the record is complete.

City and county

Permit portals, planning records, agency pages, and public record indexes where searchable.

Property context

Assessor, parcel, APN, map layer, and jurisdiction clues where available.

Risk notes

Items that need manual confirmation before money, scope, or timeline commitments move forward.

Section 2

What We Check

The exact source mix depends on the address and the records available for that jurisdiction.

  • Address and APN confirmation where available
  • Permit history
  • Planning and zoning records
  • Parcel and assessor records
  • Public mapping layers where available
  • Housing or code records where searchable
  • Manual confirmation flags
Section 3

What You Receive

Permit Review Plus is structured so a buyer, contractor, agent, owner, investor, or project lead can scan the first page and know what needs attention next.

01

Decision Snapshot

02

Official Source Checklist

03

PermitPulse Research Log

04

Evidence screenshots or PDFs where available

05

What was found

06

What was not found

07

What needs manual confirmation

08

Recommended next move

Section 4

How PermitPulse Signals Fit In

The Red Tape Index, Radar, and Top Permits pages are public-record signal pages. They help show where permit activity, friction, and high-value records may be appearing. They are not official rankings and do not replace a property-specific review.

Red Tape Index Radar Top Permits Sample Report Who It Helps
Section 5

What It Is Not

PermitPulse is careful because public records can be incomplete, delayed, inconsistent, or difficult to interpret without agency confirmation.

PermitPulse is not:

  • a legal opinion
  • an architectural opinion
  • a title report
  • a survey
  • a code determination
  • a permit expeditor service
  • a permit approval guarantee
  • a guarantee that city records are complete
Next step

Start with one address.

Send the address and what decision you are trying to make. PermitPulse will keep the review grounded in public records, source limits, and manual confirmation flags.
